exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: In the 1850s, doctor John Snow mapped cholera cases in London and noticed they clustered around a single water pump on Broad Street. He convinced authorities to remove the pump handle, and the outbreak slowed dramatically. This was decades before germ theory was widely accepted—most doctors still believed diseases were caused by bad air.

questions.question: What made Snow's approach revolutionary for his time?
options: He used data and evidence to trace disease to its source rather than relying on popular belief
He invented the first water pump
He proved that bad air causes cholera
He was the first doctor in London
questions.correctOption: He used data and evidence to trace disease to its source rather than relying on popular belief

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: The International Space Station orbits Earth at about 28,000 kilometers per hour, completing one full orbit every 90 minutes. Astronauts aboard experience sixteen sunrises and sunsets every day. In the microgravity environment, muscles and bones weaken quickly, so astronauts must exercise at least two hours daily to maintain their health.

questions.question: Why would returning to Earth after months in space be physically challenging for astronauts?
options: Because their muscles and bones have weakened in microgravity despite exercise
Because Earth's air is different from the station's
Because they forget how to walk after 90 minutes
Because the station moves too fast to slow down
questions.correctOption: Because their muscles and bones have weakened in microgravity despite exercise

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Fireflies produce light through a chemical reaction in their abdomens involving a substance called luciferin. Unlike a light bulb, which wastes most of its energy as heat, a firefly's light is nearly 100 percent efficient—almost no energy is lost as heat. Scientists are studying this process to develop more efficient lighting technology for human use.

questions.question: Why would studying firefly light be useful for developing human technology?
options: Because firefly light is nearly 100 percent efficient, which could inspire more energy-efficient lighting
Because fireflies are easier to catch than other insects
Because human light bulbs use luciferin
Because firefly light is brighter than any human invention
questions.correctOption: Because firefly light is nearly 100 percent efficient, which could inspire more energy-efficient lighting

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: The judge announced the winner of the spelling bee. Everyone clapped for Maria. In the back row, her older brother David clapped the hardest, even though he had lost in the first round of the same competition five years ago. He had been the one quizzing Maria with flashcards every night for three months.

questions.question: Why is David's reaction especially meaningful given his own history with the spelling bee?
acceptableAnswers: he turned his own failure into his sister's success
he used his experience to help Maria win
instead of being bitter about losing he helped his sister
his loss motivated him to help Maria succeed
questions.sampleAnswer: David's reaction is meaningful because rather than being bitter about his own first-round loss, he channeled that experience into helping his sister succeed by studying with her for months.

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: The moving boxes were finally unpacked. Twelve-year-old Omar stood in his new room staring at blank walls. He reached into the last box and pulled out a framed photo of four smiling kids standing in front of a school. He hung it on the wall directly above his bed and stepped back. The room felt a little less empty.

questions.question: What does Omar's choice of what to hang first reveal about what he misses most?
acceptableAnswers: his friends are the most important thing to him
he values his friendships from his old school
he misses the people from his previous home
questions.sampleAnswer: Omar's choice to hang the photo of his friends first shows that he misses his friendships most of all, and their image makes his new room feel more like home.

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Grandmother sat in her rocking chair, eyes closed, listening to the rain. 'This is the same sound,' she said. 'Sixty years ago, on my wedding day, it rained just like this. Everyone said it was bad luck.' She opened her eyes and smiled. 'They were wrong about that.'

questions.question: What does Grandmother mean when she says 'They were wrong about that'?
acceptableAnswers: her marriage was happy despite the rain
the rain was not bad luck after all
she had a good life with her spouse
the superstition about rain on a wedding day was wrong for her
questions.sampleAnswer: Grandmother means that despite the superstition about rain being bad luck, her marriage turned out to be a happy one, proving that the rain was not an omen.
